name: jira-self-hosted
title: Jira (Self-Hosted)
description: Create, access, and modify issues and sprints.
author: ernest0n

preferences.name: copyURLtoClipboard
preferences.description: If checked, copies the newly created issue URL to the clipboard.
preferences.label: Copy created issue URL to clipboard

commands.name: open-issues
commands.title: Open Issues
commands.subtitle: Jira
commands.description: View your open issues with optional Active Sprint, All Sprints, and Backlog sections.

preferences.name: showActiveSprintIssues
preferences.title: Show Active Sprint
preferences.description: Show only your open issues in currently active sprints as a dedicated section.
preferences.label: Show Active Sprint section

preferences.name: showAllSprintsIssues
preferences.title: Show All Sprints
preferences.description: Show your open issues that belong to any sprint, split into sections by sprint name.
preferences.label: Show All Sprints section

preferences.name: showBacklogIssues
preferences.title: Show Backlog
preferences.description: Show your open issues that are not assigned to any sprint in a Backlog section.
preferences.label: Show Backlog section

commands.name: reported-issues
commands.title: Reported Issues
commands.subtitle: Jira
commands.description: View and modify issues that were reported by you.

commands.name: watched-issues
commands.title: Watched Issues
commands.subtitle: Jira
commands.description: View and modify issues that you are watching.

commands.name: active-sprints
commands.title: Active Sprints
commands.subtitle: Jira
commands.description: View and modify issues of active sprints.

commands.name: my-filters
commands.title: My Filters
commands.subtitle: Jira
commands.description: View and modify issues of your saved filters.

preferences.name: sortByFavourite
preferences.description: If checked, any filters marked as favourite in Jira will be displayed first in the filter selection dropdown.
preferences.title: Favourite Filters
preferences.label: Show favourite filters ahead of others

commands.name: recently-updated-issues
commands.title: Recently Updated Issues
commands.subtitle: Jira
commands.description: View and modify issues that have recently been updated.

commands.name: search-issues
commands.title: Search Issues
commands.subtitle: Jira
commands.description: Search issues globally in all projects.

commands.name: open-from-clipboard
commands.title: Open Issue from Clipboard
commands.subtitle: Jira
commands.description: Open an issue by its key from the clipboard.

preferences.name: siteUrl
preferences.title: Jira Base URL
preferences.description: Your Jira Server / Data Center URL, including the context path if used (e.g. https://jira.company.com or https://company.com/jira)

preferences.name: token
preferences.title: Password / API Token
preferences.description: Your Jira password or Personal Access Token

preferences.name: authType
preferences.title: Auth Type
preferences.description: Basic encodes username + password/token as Base64. Bearer sends a Personal Access Token directly (Jira Data Center 9+).

preferences.name: insecureSSL
preferences.title: SSL
preferences.label: Trust self-signed SSL certificates
preferences.description: Disable SSL certificate verification. Use only for internal instances with self-signed certificates.

preferences.name: open_in
preferences.title: Open Issues in
preferences.description: Select the browser or application to open issues with.

preferences.name: branch_name
preferences.title: Git Branch Name
preferences.description:
The format for copying Git branch names.
Available keys: {issueKey} {issueSummary} {issueSummaryShort} {issueType} {projectKey}

preferences.name: showTextareaCustomFields
preferences.title: Issue detail
preferences.description: When enabled, multi-line custom fields (Jira textarea type) are appended to the issue detail markdown. When disabled, those fields are hidden.
preferences.label: Show textarea custom fields on issue detail

tools.name: get-myself
tools.title: Get Myself
tools.description: Get the currently authenticated Jira user

tools.name: assign-issue
tools.title: Assign Issue
tools.description: Assign a Jira issue to a user or unassign it

tools.name: get-issue
tools.title: Get Issue
tools.description: Get detailed information about a Jira issue

ai.instructions:
- Always format object titles and names as markdown links using the object's URL. Example: Instead of saying "Issue **Implement user profile page** has been created', say "Issue [Implement user profile page](https://jira.company.com/browse/MOB-163) has been created"
- Do not use names or titles as IDs in the system. Use the actual ID of the object
- When there is no exact match of issue titles with the provided name, analyze the input and titles of existing objects and use such objects only if the meaning is the same. Do not use objects that are not related to the input
- Do not set any fields when creating issues if the user didn't specify any. For example, if the user didn't specify a due date, do not set it.
- Jira users are identified by their username (the 'name' field), not by an accountId. Use 'name' when referencing users in JQL or when assigning issues.

expected.meetsCriteria: Includes markdown link to created issue

fields.summary: Fix LLM model in AI extensions
Fix website bug in AI Extensions

evals.input: @jira Which issues were completed by Thomas last week?

expected.meetsCriteria: Lists completed issues from last week
